project.name: agent-engine-platform
project.description: 一站式 AI Agent 构建、管理与运行平台。后端 FastAPI + Python 3.11，前端 Next.js 14 + React 18 + Ant Design，Docker Compose 编排。

nodes.name: api.ts
nodes.summary: Central API client module wrapping all backend HTTP endpoints for agents, knowledge bases, models, conversations, tools, feedback, annotations, and audit logs.

nodes.name: ApiClient

nodes.name: auth.test.ts
nodes.summary: Test suite for the auth store verifying login, logout, token persistence, and unauthenticated state transitions.

nodes.name: auth.ts
nodes.summary: Zustand auth store managing user authentication state, JWT token persistence via cookies, and login/logout actions.

nodes.name: chat.ts
nodes.summary: Zustand chat store managing conversation messages, input state, and message sending with streaming support.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Shared TypeScript type definitions for agents, knowledge bases, conversations, tools, models, feedback, audit logs, and workflow nodes used across the frontend.

nodes.name: knowledge.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI router exposing CRUD endpoints for knowledge bases and document upload. Handles file validation, parser selection by extension, document chunking, and segment persistence.

nodes.name: upload_document
nodes.summary: Handles multi-step document upload: validates the knowledge base, checks file extension, saves the file to disk, parses content, chunks text, and persists document segments to the database.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel re-exporting DocumentChunker from the chunker module.

nodes.name: chunker.py
nodes.summary: Text chunking strategies for RAG document processing, supporting recursive, semantic, parent-child, and QA-pair chunking modes with configurable token limits and overlap.

nodes.name: DocumentChunker
nodes.summary: Core chunker supporting recursive, semantic, parent-child, and QA-pair strategies. Dispatches to the appropriate algorithm based on the configured mode.

nodes.name: ParentChildChunker
nodes.summary: Wraps DocumentChunker to produce parent-child chunk pairs with shared parent IDs for hierarchical retrieval contexts.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel re-exporting KnowledgeGraphBuilder from the graph_builder module.

nodes.name: graph_builder.py
nodes.summary: Knowledge graph construction engine that extracts entities and relations from document chunks using LLM-based or heuristic profiling, with incremental update and cancellation support.

nodes.name: KnowledgeGraphBuilder
nodes.summary: Builds and incrementally updates a knowledge graph from document chunks. Uses LLM-based entity/relation extraction with heuristic fallback, concurrency-limited processing, and progress callbacks.

nodes.name: base.py
nodes.summary: Abstract base class for document parsers providing path validation, safe parse with error handling, and extension-based file type checking.

nodes.name: BaseDocumentParser
nodes.summary: Abstract base parser that validates file paths (resolving symlinks, checking existence and extension) and wraps parse() calls in safe_parse() with structured error handling.

nodes.name: excel_parser.py
nodes.summary: Excel (.xlsx) parser using openpyxl to extract sheet names, headers, and row data as structured text.

nodes.name: ExcelParser
nodes.summary: Parses Excel workbooks sheet-by-sheet, extracting headers and cell values into tab-separated text lines per sheet.

nodes.name: pdf_parser.py
nodes.summary: PDF parser using PyMuPDF (fitz) with OCR fallback for scanned pages, table extraction, and page-by-page text output.

nodes.name: PDFParser
nodes.summary: Parses PDF documents using PyMuPDF with optional OCR via an adapter for image-based pages, extracting text, tables, and metadata per page.

nodes.name: ppt_parser.py
nodes.summary: PowerPoint (.pptx) parser using python-pptx to extract slide text content.

nodes.name: PPTParser
nodes.summary: Parses PowerPoint presentations extracting text from slide shapes, joined with slide separators.

nodes.name: text_parser.py
nodes.summary: Multi-format plain text parser handling .txt, .csv (via csv.reader), .html (via BeautifulSoup), .md, and .json files.

nodes.name: TextParser
nodes.summary: Parses plain text, CSV, HTML (via BeautifulSoup), Markdown, and JSON files based on file extension.

nodes.name: word_parser.py
nodes.summary: Word (.docx) parser using python-docx to extract paragraphs and tables as structured text.

nodes.name: WordParser
nodes.summary: Parses Word documents extracting paragraph text and table data with metadata.

nodes.name: rag_pipeline.py
nodes.summary: Main RAG pipeline orchestrating embedding, multi-strategy retrieval (naive, LightRAG, legacy hybrid), reranking, graph context enrichment, and LLM-based answer generation.

nodes.name: RAGPipeline
nodes.summary: End-to-end RAG pipeline with naive, LightRAG, and legacy retrieval modes. Integrates vector search, full-text search, graph retrieval, RRF fusion, reranking, citation enrichment, and LLM generation.

nodes.name: reranker.py
nodes.summary: Document reranker using an LLM adapter to reorder search results by relevance score with top-k truncation.

nodes.name: Reranker
nodes.summary: Reranks documents using an optional LLM-based reranking adapter, falling back to identity when no adapter is configured.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel re-exporting DualLevelRetriever, GraphRetriever, and HybridRetriever.

nodes.name: dual_retriever.py
nodes.summary: Dual-level retriever implementing LightRAG-style retrieval with naive, local (entity), global (theme), and hybrid modes, using keyword extraction and RRF fusion across graph and vector stores.

nodes.name: DualLevelRetriever
nodes.summary: LightRAG-inspired retriever with low-level (entity) and high-level (theme) search, keyword extraction via LLM or heuristics, and RRF fusion of multiple retrieval streams.

nodes.name: graph_retriever.py
nodes.summary: Graph-based retriever performing local (entity neighborhood) and global (hub node) retrieval with weighted merge of results.

nodes.name: GraphRetriever
nodes.summary: Retrieves context from a knowledge graph using local entity-neighborhood expansion and global hub-node discovery, with configurable search modes and weighted result merging.

nodes.name: retriever.py
nodes.summary: Legacy hybrid retriever combining vector and full-text search with RRF merge, delegating core logic to RAGPipeline internals.

nodes.name: HybridRetriever
nodes.summary: Hybrid retriever wrapping RAGPipeline's legacy retrieval strategy with RRF merge and citation enrichment, providing a simplified interface for vector + full-text + graph search.

nodes.name: milvus_store.py
nodes.summary: Milvus vector store adapter providing async CRUD operations (create collection, insert, search, delete) with input sanitization and per-tenant collection isolation.

nodes.name: MilvusVectorStore
nodes.summary: Async Milvus vector store with collection auto-creation, IVF_FLAT indexing, embedding search, and safe ID-based deletion with regex validation.

nodes.name: knowledge_service.py
nodes.summary: Platform-level service managing knowledge base lifecycle (CRUD) with multi-tenant isolation, vector store collection provisioning, and Elasticsearch index management.

nodes.name: KnowledgeBaseService
nodes.summary: Service layer for knowledge base management handling create, get, list, and delete operations with tenant-scoped database queries and backing vector/ES store provisioning.

nodes.name: test_knowledge_engine.py
nodes.summary: Comprehensive integration tests covering all document parsers (PDF, Word, PPT, Excel, Text), path traversal security, chunking strategies (recursive, semantic, QA-pair), HyDE retrieval, reranker, RAG pipeline, and knowledge graph builder with cancellation.

nodes.name: test_vector_store.py
nodes.summary: Integration tests for MilvusVectorStore covering collection creation, insert, search, delete, tenant isolation, and HybridRetriever fallback strategies.

nodes.name: test_knowledge_engine.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for chunker (recursive, semantic, overlap), parser path validation and safe parse, text/CSV/Markdown parsing, hybrid retriever RRF merge, and graph builder label validation and cancellation.

nodes.name: test_rag_pipeline.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for RAGPipeline covering LLM-based generation, no-LLM fallback, empty result handling, missing vector store, and graph context enrichment.

nodes.name: exceptions.py
nodes.summary: Defines the platform-wide exception hierarchy with AgentEngineError as the base class and 10 domain-specific subclasses covering model routing, rate limiting, knowledge base, document, tool, permission, and tenant errors.
nodes.languageNotes: Flat exception hierarchy using Python Exception subclasses with docstrings for each domain error type.

nodes.name: whisper_asr.py
nodes.summary: ASR adapter for Whisper-based speech recognition, sending multipart audio files via httpx and returning structured ASRResult objects.

nodes.name: base.py
nodes.summary: Core abstractions for the model engine layer: defines ModelType/LLMCapability/EmbeddingCapability enums, LLMModelConfig/EmbeddingModelConfig dataclasses, and abstract base adapters (BaseLLMAdapter, BaseEmbeddingAdapter, BaseRerankAdapter, BaseASRAdapter) with async chat/embed/rerank/transcribe interfaces.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Python dataclasses for config objects and ABC-style abstract base classes with async method stubs for adapter contracts.

nodes.name: cost_tracker.py
nodes.summary: Tracks per-tenant model usage costs by looking up ModelConfigModel pricing, computing cost from token counts, and persisting UsageLogModel records via SQLAlchemy async sessions.

nodes.name: openai_embedding.py
nodes.summary: OpenAI-compatible embedding adapter that sends text via httpx to the /embeddings endpoint and returns parsed embedding vectors.

nodes.name: anthropic.py
nodes.summary: Anthropic Claude LLM adapter implementing chat and streaming via the Messages API, extracting system prompts, handling content blocks, and constructing LLMResponse/TokenUsage objects.

nodes.name: custom_openai.py
nodes.summary: Thin adapter for OpenAI-compatible APIs (DeepSeek, Qwen, Moonshot, vLLM) that inherits all behavior from OpenAIAdapter without modification.

nodes.name: ollama.py
nodes.summary: Ollama local LLM adapter implementing chat and streaming via the Ollama REST API with NDJSON streaming support.

nodes.name: openai.py
nodes.summary: OpenAI LLM adapter providing chat, streaming, and function calling capabilities via the OpenAI Chat Completions API with httpx.

nodes.name: monitor.py
nodes.summary: In-memory model performance monitoring that tracks per-model request counts, latency, and error rates using a defaultdict-backed metrics store.

nodes.name: presets.py
nodes.summary: Pre-configured LLM and embedding model catalogs covering OpenAI, Anthropic, DeepSeek, Qwen, Jina, and BGE models with pricing and capability metadata.
nodes.languageNotes: Module-level constants defining model catalogs with pricing and capability metadata, used as seed data for the model registry.

nodes.name: cohere_rerank.py
nodes.summary: Cohere rerank adapter that sends documents and queries to the Cohere rerank API and returns scored, sorted RerankResult objects.

nodes.name: router.py
nodes.summary: Model routing layer with circuit breaker pattern, weighted and round-robin provider selection, and health-based endpoint filtering for resilient multi-provider model access.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements the Circuit Breaker pattern with closed/open/half-open states and timeout-based recovery, combined with weighted random and round-robin selection strategies.

nodes.name: agent_service.py
nodes.summary: Full CRUD service for agent management with tenant-scoped operations, pagination, and SQLAlchemy-based persistence of AgentModel entities.

nodes.name: tenant_service.py
nodes.summary: Tenant management service providing CRUD, agent quota enforcement, feature flag checks, and quota/feature updates with custom QuotaExceededError and FeatureDisabledError exceptions.

nodes.name: common.py
nodes.summary: Shared data transfer objects for the entire platform: defines 15 dataclass-based schemas covering LLM responses, token usage, search/rerank results, ASR, safety, RAG, tool execution, workflow, memory, knowledge graph entities, and provider endpoints.

nodes.name: test_model_engine.py
nodes.summary: Comprehensive integration test suite with 20 test classes covering all model adapters (OpenAI, Anthropic, Ollama, DeepSeek, embedding, rerank, ASR), router behavior (weighted routing, health filtering, circuit breaker, fallback), cost calculation, budget alerts, and usage reporting.

nodes.name: test_common_schemas.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for all 15 common schema dataclasses, verifying field assignment and defaults for TokenUsage, LLMResponse, SearchResult, RerankResult, ASRResult, SafetyResult, RAGResponse, ToolResult, WorkflowResult, MemoryContext, Entity, Relation, and ProviderEndpoint.

nodes.name: test_core.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for core module components: exception hierarchy, password hashing/verification, JWT token creation/decoding, and AES encryption/decryption.

nodes.name: test_cost_tracker.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for CostTracker covering cost calculation with pricing configs, zero-token edge cases, no-config fallbacks, and usage report aggregation with empty databases.

nodes.name: test_exceptions.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for all 11 exception classes verifying instantiation, message handling, and inheritance from the base AgentEngineError.

nodes.name: test_model_engine.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for model engine core components: ModelType/LLMCapability/EmbeddingCapability enums, LLMModelConfig/EmbeddingModelConfig dataclasses, preset models, CircuitBreaker state transitions, ModelRouter endpoint registration and selection, CostTracker, and ModelMonitor.

nodes.name: test_tenant_service.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for TenantService covering agent quota checks (within limit, exceeded, no tenant), feature flag enforcement (enabled, disabled, not configured, no tenant), and feature updates.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: API v1 router barrel file that aggregates all endpoint sub-routers (agents, auth, chat, conversations, etc.) into a unified v1 API prefix.
nodes.languageNotes: Python package barrel using FastAPI APIRouter includes to compose all v1 endpoints.

nodes.name: agents.py
nodes.summary: REST API endpoints for agent CRUD operations — create, read, list, update, publish, and delete agents — with RBAC permission checks.

nodes.name: audit.py
nodes.summary: Audit log query endpoint with multi-field filtering (action, resource type, user, date range) and paginated results for compliance tracking.

nodes.name: auth.py
nodes.summary: Authentication endpoints handling login with rate limiting, current user info, and SSO provider CRUD — integrates password verification, JWT token issuance, and RBAC.
nodes.languageNotes: Includes an in-memory _LoginRateLimiter class with Redis-backed sliding window for IP-based brute-force protection.

nodes.name: chat.py
nodes.summary: Chat completion and streaming endpoints integrating safety checks, LLM adapter calls, conversation persistence, and Server-Sent Events for real-time responses.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements SSE (Server-Sent Events) streaming via EventSourceResponse with async generator for token-by-token LLM output delivery.

nodes.name: conversations.py
nodes.summary: Conversation management endpoints — list, create, get, delete conversations, retrieve and add messages — delegating to ConversationService.

nodes.name: feedbacks.py
nodes.summary: Feedback and annotation endpoints for message quality tracking — create/update feedback, get statistics, and manage message annotations.

nodes.name: memory.py
nodes.summary: Memory context API endpoints for retrieving, searching, and clearing agent memory contexts scoped to conversations.

nodes.name: models.py
nodes.summary: Model provider and configuration management endpoints — CRUD for LLM providers, model configs, default model selection, delegating to ModelService.

nodes.name: run_migrations_offline
nodes.summary: Runs Alembic migrations in offline mode using SQL script generation without database connection.

nodes.name: do_run_migrations
nodes.summary: Executes migration context configuration and runs migrations within an active connection.

nodes.name: run_async_migrations
nodes.summary: Runs migrations using an async database engine with connection lifecycle management.

nodes.name: create_agent
nodes.summary: Creates a new agent with RBAC permission check, delegating to AgentService.

nodes.name: get_agent
nodes.summary: Retrieves a single agent by ID with tenant-scoped access control.

nodes.name: list_agents
nodes.summary: Lists agents with pagination support, scoped to the current user's tenant.

nodes.name: update_agent
nodes.summary: Updates an existing agent's configuration with RBAC permission enforcement.

nodes.name: publish_agent
nodes.summary: Publishes an agent, making it available for use after RBAC check.

nodes.name: delete_agent
nodes.summary: Deletes an agent by ID with RBAC permission enforcement.

nodes.name: list_operation_logs
nodes.summary: Queries operation audit logs with multi-field filtering (action, resource type, user, date range) and paginated results.

nodes.name: login
nodes.summary: Authenticates user credentials against stored hash, issues JWT access token on success.

nodes.name: list_sso_providers
nodes.summary: Lists SSO/OAuth providers configured for the current tenant with metadata serialization.

nodes.name: create_sso_provider
nodes.summary: Creates a new SSO/OAuth provider with RBAC permission check and credential storage.

nodes.name: delete_sso_provider
nodes.summary: Deletes an SSO/OAuth provider by ID with RBAC enforcement.

nodes.name: _LoginRateLimiter
nodes.summary: IP-based login rate limiter using Redis sorted sets as a sliding window counter to prevent brute-force attacks.

nodes.name: _get_agent
nodes.summary: Helper to fetch and validate an agent by ID and tenant scope, raising 404 if not found.

nodes.name: chat_completions
nodes.summary: Handles non-streaming chat completions with safety checks, LLM invocation, and message persistence.

nodes.name: chat_stream
nodes.summary: Handles streaming chat via SSE with safety checks, async LLM token streaming, and message persistence.

nodes.name: ChatRequest
nodes.summary: Pydantic request model for chat API carrying agent_id, messages, conversation_id, and stream flag.

nodes.name: list_conversations
nodes.summary: Lists conversations with pagination, optionally filtered by agent_id, via ConversationService.

nodes.name: create_conversation
nodes.summary: Creates a new conversation via ConversationService.

nodes.name: get_conversation
nodes.summary: Retrieves a single conversation by ID with tenant-scoped access.

nodes.name: delete_conversation
nodes.summary: Deletes a conversation by ID via ConversationService.

nodes.name: get_messages
nodes.summary: Retrieves all messages for a conversation with existence check.

nodes.name: add_message
nodes.summary: Appends a new message to a conversation via ConversationService.

nodes.name: create_feedback
nodes.summary: Creates a feedback record for a message with upsert logic and tenant scoping.

nodes.name: update_feedback
nodes.summary: Updates an existing feedback record's rating and comment.

nodes.name: get_feedback_stats
nodes.summary: Computes aggregate feedback statistics (counts by rating, average) for an agent or conversation.

nodes.name: create_annotation
nodes.summary: Creates a message annotation with content and metadata.

nodes.name: list_annotations
nodes.summary: Lists annotations for a message with pagination support.

nodes.name: get_memory_context
nodes.summary: Retrieves the memory context for a conversation from the memory engine.

nodes.name: search_memory
nodes.summary: Performs semantic search across agent memory for relevant context snippets.

nodes.name: clear_memory
nodes.summary: Clears all memory entries for a conversation or agent scope.

nodes.name: create_provider
nodes.summary: Creates a new LLM model provider via ModelService.

nodes.name: list_providers
nodes.summary: Lists all model providers for the current tenant.

nodes.name: delete_provider
nodes.summary: Deletes a model provider by ID.

nodes.name: create_model_config
nodes.summary: Creates a new model configuration (model name, parameters) for a provider.

nodes.name: list_model_configs
nodes.summary: Lists model configurations for a given provider.

nodes.name: set_default_model
nodes.summary: Sets a model configuration as the default for its provider.

nodes.name: delete_model_config
nodes.summary: Deletes a model configuration entry.

nodes.name: config.py
nodes.summary: Central application settings module using pydantic-settings, defining all configuration parameters for database, Redis, Celery, Milvus, Neo4j, Elasticsearch, JWT authentication, CORS, rate limiting, uploads, workflows, and safety checks.

nodes.name: Settings
nodes.summary: Pydantic BaseSettings class holding all application configuration with validation and defaults for database URLs, Redis, Celery broker, vector store, graph store, search engine, JWT secrets, CORS, rate limits, upload constraints, workflow timeouts, and safety toggles.

nodes.name: audit.py
nodes.summary: Audit logging middleware that intercepts HTTP requests, extracts resource and action metadata, and persists operation logs to the database for compliance tracking.

nodes.name: AuditLogMiddleware
nodes.summary: Starlette middleware that intercepts non-health-check HTTP requests, extracts resource/action info, captures request details and response status, and writes audit log entries to the database via OperationLogModel.

nodes.name: logging.py
nodes.summary: Structured JSON logging infrastructure with sensitive data filtering, providing a StructuredFormatter and convenience functions for application-wide log setup and named logger creation.

nodes.name: StructuredFormatter
nodes.summary: Custom log formatter that outputs structured JSON with timestamp, level, message, and optional extra fields like request_id and tenant_id, using sensitive key filtering.

nodes.name: setup_logging
nodes.summary: Initializes the root logger with StructuredFormatter, sets appropriate levels for uvicorn and sqlalchemy loggers.

nodes.name: middleware.py
nodes.summary: Request ID middleware that assigns a unique X-Request-ID header to each incoming request and logs request processing time for distributed tracing support.

nodes.name: RequestIdMiddleware
nodes.summary: Starlette middleware that ensures every request carries a unique X-Request-ID (generating one if absent), measures processing duration, and logs request completion details.

nodes.name: rate_limiter.py
nodes.summary: Token-bucket and sliding-window rate limiter backed by Redis sorted sets, providing both request-per-minute and tokens-per-minute limiting with a FastAPI dependency for easy endpoint integration.

nodes.name: RateLimiter
nodes.summary: Redis-backed rate limiter using sorted sets for sliding window tracking, supporting both request count (check) and token usage (check_tokens/record_tokens) limiting with graceful degradation when Redis is unavailable.

nodes.name: rate_limit_dependency
nodes.summary: FastAPI dependency that extracts the client IP and enforces per-minute rate limiting, raising HTTP 429 when the limit is exceeded.

nodes.name: redis.py
nodes.summary: Async Redis client lifecycle management providing a singleton aioredis connection initialized from app config with graceful shutdown.

nodes.name: security.py
nodes.summary: Core security utilities for password hashing (bcrypt), JWT token creation and decoding, and symmetric encryption (Fernet) of sensitive data like API keys.

nodes.name: create_access_token
nodes.summary: Creates a signed JWT access token with configurable expiration using the application secret key and algorithm.

nodes.name: encrypt
nodes.summary: Encrypts plaintext using Fernet symmetric encryption with a derived key from the application encryption key.

nodes.name: decrypt
nodes.summary: Decrypts Fernet-encrypted ciphertext back to plaintext using the application encryption key.

nodes.name: main.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI application entry point that configures CORS, registers middleware (request ID, audit, HTTPS redirect), mounts API v1 routes, sets up error handlers, manages startup/shutdown lifecycle (scheduler, Redis, tools), and exposes health and documentation endpoints.

nodes.name: startup_tasks
nodes.summary: Async startup handler that initializes built-in tools, creates necessary directories, and starts the background scheduler service.

nodes.name: shutdown_tasks
nodes.summary: Async shutdown handler that stops the background scheduler and closes the Redis connection gracefully.

nodes.name: health
nodes.summary: Health check endpoint that verifies database connectivity, Redis ping, and Milvus connection, returning component-level status for monitoring.

nodes.name: model_service.py
nodes.summary: Service layer for managing LLM model providers and model configurations, handling CRUD operations with encrypted API key storage and tenant-scoped data isolation.

nodes.name: ModelService
nodes.summary: Service class providing CRUD for model providers and model configs, encrypting provider API keys at rest, and supporting set-default model selection with tenant isolation.

nodes.name: celery_app.py
nodes.summary: Celery application instance initialization using configuration from the shared Settings module, defining broker URL, result backend, concurrency, and task time limits.

nodes.name: cleanup_tasks.py
nodes.summary: Scheduled Celery tasks for system maintenance: cleaning expired memory caches, purging old temporary files, removing inactive sessions/conversations, and pruning stale audit log entries.

nodes.name: cleanup_expired_sessions
nodes.summary: Celery task that removes conversations and associated messages for sessions inactive beyond the configured threshold, using async database queries.

nodes.name: cleanup_old_audit_logs
nodes.summary: Celery task that batch-deletes OperationLogModel entries older than the retention period using paginated database queries to avoid memory pressure.

nodes.name: document_tasks.py
nodes.summary: Celery tasks for document processing pipeline: parsing files by type (PDF, Word, PPT, Excel, text), chunking content, embedding segments, and managing task progress, cancellation, and dead-letter retry.

nodes.name: RetryableTask
nodes.summary: Celery task base class that implements automatic retry with dead-letter tracking, logging failures and supporting manual retry of failed tasks.

nodes.name: process_document
nodes.summary: Main document processing task that parses files by type, chunks text content, and stores segments for embedding, with progress state updates throughout the pipeline.

nodes.name: model_tasks.py
nodes.summary: Scheduled Celery tasks for LLM model operations: budget alert checking against monthly spending, model health probing via lightweight chat completions, and daily usage aggregation into ModelUsageDailyModel for reporting.

nodes.name: check_budget_alerts
nodes.summary: Celery task that aggregates monthly usage costs per tenant and logs warnings when spending exceeds warning or critical budget thresholds.

nodes.name: aggregate_usage_daily
nodes.summary: Celery task that aggregates daily usage logs into ModelUsageDailyModel entries grouped by tenant, provider, and model, creating or updating daily summary records.

nodes.name: conftest.py
nodes.summary: Pytest fixtures providing a dedicated asyncio event loop and an httpx AsyncClient wired to the FastAPI app for async test execution.

nodes.name: test_critical_flows.py
nodes.summary: End-to-end test suite covering critical user flows: login/logout, token validation, agent listing, health checks, OpenAPI documentation, expired/forged token rejection, and tenant isolation using a mock database.

nodes.name: MockDB
nodes.summary: Mock database class for E2E tests that simulates SQLAlchemy session behavior with in-memory storage for execute, add, flush, and delete operations.

nodes.name: test_auth_security.py
nodes.summary: Comprehensive integration test suite covering RBAC role enforcement, data scope isolation, SQL injection protection, rate limiting, encryption roundtrips, sensitive log filtering, and HTTPS enforcement with real database fixtures.

nodes.name: TestRBACRequireRole
nodes.summary: Integration tests verifying RBAC require_role dependency: admin-only access, role rejection, multi-role endpoints, inactive user blocking, and missing auth header handling.

nodes.name: TestRateLimiter
nodes.summary: Integration tests for rate limiting with mock Redis: under-limit allowance, over-limit blocking, no-Redis graceful degradation, and FastAPI dependency integration.

nodes.name: TestEncryption
nodes.summary: Integration tests verifying encrypt/decrypt roundtrips, ciphertext uniqueness, special character handling, tampered ciphertext rejection, and provider API key encryption at rest.

nodes.name: test_api_health.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests verifying the health check, Swagger docs, and OpenAPI spec endpoints return correct status codes and content.

nodes.name: test_auth.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for authentication utilities: JWT token creation/decoding, expiry validation, password hash roundtrips, require_role dependency factory, and tenant ID extraction.

nodes.name: test_rate_limiter.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for RateLimiter covering request-per-minute and tokens-per-minute limiting with mock Redis, including edge cases at exact limits and graceful no-Redis behavior.

nodes.name: test_security.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for security utilities: password hashing, JWT token roundtrips, and Fernet encrypt/decrypt operations including wrong-key rejection.

nodes.name: workflows.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI router providing full CRUD and execution management for workflows, including listing, creating, updating, deleting, running, versioning, and rollback operations.

nodes.name: list_workflows
nodes.summary: Paginated listing of workflows filtered by tenant, returning total count and workflow details.

nodes.name: create_workflow
nodes.summary: Creates a new workflow definition with tenant-scoped ownership and persists it to the database.

nodes.name: update_workflow
nodes.summary: Updates an existing workflow and snapshots a version via WorkflowVersionService before applying changes.

nodes.name: run_workflow
nodes.summary: Triggers workflow execution by looking up the workflow and delegating to WorkflowExecutionService.

nodes.name: list_executions
nodes.summary: Paginated listing of workflow executions for a given workflow, scoped by tenant.

nodes.name: rollback_workflow_version
nodes.summary: Rolls back a workflow to a specific version by restoring the snapshot from WorkflowVersionService.

nodes.name: auth.py
nodes.summary: Authentication and authorization dependency module providing JWT/API token verification, role-based access control, and tenant-scoped data filtering for FastAPI endpoints.

nodes.name: _authenticate_token
nodes.summary: Core token authentication logic that attempts JWT verification first, then falls back to API token lookup in the database.

nodes.name: get_current_user
nodes.summary: FastAPI dependency that extracts and validates the current user from the Authorization header, attaching tenant context.

nodes.name: get_current_user_with_permissions
nodes.summary: Extended user dependency that also fetches the user's permission set from the RBAC cache/database.

nodes.name: require_role
nodes.summary: Factory returning a FastAPI dependency that enforces the user has a specific role.

nodes.name: require_data_scope
nodes.summary: Factory returning a dependency that enforces a specific data scope level (tenant, department, or personal) on the current user.

nodes.name: apply_data_scope
nodes.summary: Applies data scope filtering to a SQLAlchemy query based on the user's scope level, adding tenant, department, or user-level WHERE clauses.

nodes.name: database.py
nodes.summary: Database engine and session factory configuration using SQLAlchemy async, providing a FastAPI dependency that yields auto-committing sessions.

nodes.name: get_db
nodes.summary: FastAPI dependency that yields an async SQLAlchemy session with automatic commit/rollback handling.

nodes.name: rbac.py
nodes.summary: Role-based access control module implementing permission checking, role initialization, Redis-cached permission queries, and a FastAPI dependency factory for permission enforcement.

nodes.name: init_default_roles
nodes.summary: Seeds the database with default roles (owner, admin, contributor, viewer) and their associated permissions.

nodes.name: get_user_permissions
nodes.summary: Retrieves a user's permission set, checking Redis cache first and falling back to database query.

nodes.name: require_permission
nodes.summary: Public API that creates a FastAPI dependency enforcing a specific resource:action permission on the current user.

nodes.name: scheduler.py
nodes.summary: Cron-based and event-driven scheduler service using a singleton pattern to manage trigger registration, execution, and background polling.

nodes.name: SchedulerService
nodes.summary: Singleton scheduler service that manages cron triggers and event-based triggers with background polling loop for cron evaluation.

nodes.name: webhook_dispatcher.py
nodes.summary: Webhook delivery module handling HMAC signature computation, HTTP delivery with retries, and event-type-based dispatch routing to subscribed webhooks.

nodes.name: deliver_webhook
nodes.summary: Delivers a webhook payload to a URL with HMAC signature, exponential backoff retries, and SSRF URL validation.

nodes.name: dispatch_webhook
nodes.summary: Routes a webhook event to all matching subscribed webhooks based on event type patterns.

nodes.name: base.py
nodes.summary: Central SQLAlchemy ORM model definitions for the entire platform, containing 46 model classes covering tenants, users, agents, workflows, conversations, knowledge bases, tools, evaluations, webhooks, and more.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses SQLAlchemy declarative base with UUID primary keys and UTC timestamps. Models follow a tenant-scoped multi-tenant pattern with relationships and cascade deletes.

nodes.name: TenantModel
nodes.summary: Top-level tenant model with quota settings, feature flags, and relationships to users, agents, knowledge bases, and other tenant-scoped resources.

nodes.name: UserModel
nodes.summary: User model with tenant association, role, department, and authentication fields.

nodes.name: AgentModel
nodes.summary: Agent configuration model storing system prompt, LLM settings, knowledge base bindings, tools, and publish status.

nodes.name: WorkflowModel
nodes.summary: Workflow definition model storing the DAG graph, variables, and tenant association.

nodes.name: WorkflowExecutionModel
nodes.summary: Workflow execution run model tracking status, current node, variables snapshot, and timing.

nodes.name: KnowledgeBaseModel
nodes.summary: Knowledge base model with embedding dimensions, vector collection name, and document count tracking.

nodes.name: RoleModel
nodes.summary: RBAC role model with name, system flag, description, and tenant association.

nodes.name: WebhookModel
nodes.summary: Webhook configuration model storing URL, secret, subscribed event types, and enabled state.

nodes.name: conversation_service.py
nodes.summary: Service layer for conversation management providing CRUD operations on conversations and their messages with database persistence.

nodes.name: ConversationService
nodes.summary: Conversation service class implementing create, get, list, message management, and delete operations using SQLAlchemy async.

nodes.name: workflow_service.py
nodes.summary: Service layer for workflow execution and version management, providing execution lifecycle tracking and version snapshot/rollback capabilities.

nodes.name: WorkflowExecutionService
nodes.summary: Manages workflow execution lifecycle including starting, tracking node status, updating execution state, and resuming paused executions.

nodes.name: WorkflowVersionService
nodes.summary: Manages workflow version snapshots including creating, listing, retrieving, and rolling back to specific versions.

nodes.name: api.py
nodes.summary: Centralized Pydantic schema definitions for all API request/response models covering auth, agents, workflows, conversations, knowledge bases, tools, evaluations, webhooks, and more.

nodes.name: conftest.py
nodes.summary: Shared pytest fixtures for integration tests providing a real async database engine, session factory, and a TestClient wired to the database.

nodes.name: test_api_integration.py
nodes.summary: Comprehensive integration test suite covering authentication, agent CRUD, chat completions (sync and SSE stream), tenant isolation, knowledge base operations, model configuration, task queue, and safety injection blocking.

nodes.name: test_performance_sla.py
nodes.summary: Performance SLA test suite verifying response time thresholds for health, auth, and agent endpoints, plus concurrency tests and SLA documentation validation.

nodes.name: test_workflow_persistence.py
nodes.summary: Integration tests validating workflow DAG execution, node status tracking, variable propagation, execution persistence, resume functionality, and tenant isolation.

nodes.name: test_data_scope.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests verifying data scope filtering applies correct tenant-level constraints and that the require_data_scope factory produces valid callables.

nodes.name: test_rbac.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests covering RBAC permission dictionary structure, default role definitions, permission checking, user permission retrieval, and Redis cache fallback behavior.

nodes.name: test_scheduler.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for cron field parsing, cron matching logic, and SchedulerService singleton behavior including trigger add/remove and event firing.

nodes.name: test_tokens.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for token generation, JWT/API token fallback authentication, token expiry handling, and permission passthrough from API tokens.

nodes.name: test_webhooks.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests covering webhook event types, HMAC signature computation, HTTP delivery with retries, and dispatch routing to subscribed webhooks.

nodes.name: ssrf.py
nodes.summary: SSRF protection utilities that validate URLs against blocked IP ranges (private/link-local/metadata) and blocked hostnames, with DNS resolution pinning to prevent TOCTOU rebinding attacks.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses ipaddress module for CIDR matching and socket.getaddrinfo for DNS resolution with IP pinning to prevent DNS rebinding.

nodes.name: is_safe_url
nodes.summary: Deprecated SSRF URL check that delegates to is_safe_url_with_ip, returning only (safe, reason) without the pinned IP.

nodes.name: is_safe_url_with_ip
nodes.summary: Validates a URL against blocked IP ranges and hostnames, resolves DNS, checks all resolved addresses, and returns the pinned IP for TOCTOU-safe connections.

nodes.name: _is_safe_ip
nodes.summary: Checks whether an IP address falls outside all blocked private/link-local/metadata CIDR ranges.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel file re-exporting ToolRegistry and ToolExecutor as the public API of the tool_engine package.

nodes.name: calculator.py
nodes.summary: Built-in calculator tool that evaluates mathematical expressions using sympy (with a restricted eval fallback), supporting arithmetic, algebra, calculus, and trigonometry.

nodes.name: _execute
nodes.summary: Evaluates a mathematical expression string using sympy with a whitelist of allowed functions (sqrt, sin, integrate, etc.), falling back to restricted eval when sympy is unavailable.

nodes.name: code_executor.py
nodes.summary: Built-in code execution tool that runs Python code in a sandboxed subprocess with AST-level import restrictions blocking dangerous modules like os, sys, subprocess, and socket.

nodes.name: _check_imports
nodes.summary: AST-based import validator that rejects code importing blocked modules (subprocess, os, socket, ctypes, etc.) to prevent sandbox escapes.

nodes.name: _execute
nodes.summary: Executes Python code in an isolated subprocess with import validation, a configurable timeout, and a stripped environment to prevent privilege escalation.

nodes.name: db_query.py
nodes.summary: Built-in read-only database query tool that validates SQL to only allow SELECT/WITH queries, strips comments, blocks multi-statement injection, and enforces row limits.

nodes.name: _is_safe_query
nodes.summary: SQL safety validator that strips comments, prevents multi-statement injection via semicolons, enforces SELECT/WITH-only prefix, and blocks forbidden keywords like INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, DROP.

nodes.name: _execute
nodes.summary: Executes a validated read-only SQL query via SQLAlchemy with enforced LIMIT clause and returns column names and row data.

nodes.name: file_ops.py
nodes.summary: Built-in file operations tool supporting read, write, list, and exists within a restricted set of allowed base directories, with path traversal protection via realpath resolution.

nodes.name: _validate_path
nodes.summary: Validates that a resolved file path falls within one of the allowed base directories to prevent path traversal attacks.

nodes.name: _execute
nodes.summary: Dispatches file read, write, list, and exists operations with path validation, file size limits (1MB), directory entry limits (200), and async executor delegation for blocking I/O.

nodes.name: http_request.py
nodes.summary: Built-in HTTP request tool that makes outbound requests with SSRF protection via URL validation, disabled redirects, and configurable timeouts.

nodes.name: _execute
nodes.summary: Executes an HTTP request after validating the URL against SSRF rules, using httpx AsyncClient with redirect following disabled.

nodes.name: web_search.py
nodes.summary: Built-in web search tool using DuckDuckGo Search, running searches in a thread executor to avoid blocking the async event loop.

nodes.name: _execute
nodes.summary: Performs a DuckDuckGo text search in a thread executor and returns results with title, URL, and snippet fields.

nodes.name: executor.py
nodes.summary: Tool execution engine that runs tools with timeout enforcement, execution logging to the database, permission checks, and support for OpenAI function-calling format from agents.

nodes.name: ToolExecutor
nodes.summary: Central tool executor class providing lifecycle-managed execution with DB logging, timeout handling, agent-format argument parsing, and execution history querying.

nodes.name: registry.py
nodes.summary: Singleton tool registry (ToolRegistry) managing tool registration, lookup, and OpenAI function-calling schema export, along with ToolDef data class and built-in tool bootstrap function.

nodes.name: ToolDef
nodes.summary: Immutable tool definition data class using __slots__ that encapsulates name, description, schema, handler, permissions, and provides OpenAI function-calling format conversion.

nodes.name: ToolRegistry
nodes.summary: Singleton registry for all tools providing register, unregister, get, list, and OpenAI schema export methods, with optional filtering by tool type or permission.

nodes.name: register_builtin_tools
nodes.summary: Bootstrap function that imports and registers all six built-in tools (web_search, calculator, code_executor, http_request, db_query, file_ops) into the global registry.

nodes.name: schema_parser.py
nodes.summary: Parses OpenAPI 3.0 specifications into ToolDef objects, generating per-operation handlers with SSRF-protected HTTP execution via httpx.

nodes.name: parse_openapi_to_tools
nodes.summary: Main entry point that iterates over all paths and HTTP methods in an OpenAPI spec, building a ToolDef per operation with generated handler, schema, and config.

nodes.name: _extract_base_url
nodes.summary: Extracts the base URL from an OpenAPI spec by checking the servers array first, then falling back to host/basePath/schemes fields.

nodes.name: _build_input_schema
nodes.summary: Converts OpenAPI operation parameters and request body into a JSON Schema object suitable for tool input validation.

nodes.name: _create_api_handler
nodes.summary: Factory that creates an async handler function for a specific API endpoint, performing SSRF validation, path parameter substitution, and HTTP request execution via httpx.

nodes.name: memory.py
nodes.summary: Core memory engine providing multi-layer memory management with short-term, long-term, working, and episodic memory subsystems, plus a summarization pipeline and a top-level MemoryEngine facade that orchestrates them all.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Redis for short-term/working memory storage with TTL-based expiration, vector store for long-term semantic search, and LLM adapter for memory extraction and summarization.

nodes.name: ShortTermMemory
nodes.summary: Redis-backed short-term message buffer with TTL-based expiration and capacity limits, storing recent conversation messages as JSON-serialized entries.

nodes.name: LongTermMemory
nodes.summary: Long-term memory subsystem that uses LLM to extract key facts from conversations, embeds them via vector embeddings, and stores them in a vector store for semantic retrieval.

nodes.name: WorkingMemory
nodes.summary: Working memory that compresses conversation context into summaries via LLM when message count exceeds a threshold, storing the summary in Redis.

nodes.name: EpisodicMemory
nodes.summary: Episodic memory that stores conversation episodes with metadata (participants, topics, sentiment) and supports semantic recall via embedding-based vector search.

nodes.name: _cosine_similarity
nodes.summary: Computes cosine similarity between two numeric vectors, used as a fallback similarity metric.

nodes.name: _extract_topics
nodes.summary: Extracts top-N topics from text using token frequency analysis with stop-word filtering.

nodes.name: SummarizationPipeline
nodes.summary: Summarization pipeline that triggers LLM-based summarization when short-term message count exceeds a threshold, with a fallback topic-extraction summarizer when LLM is unavailable.

nodes.name: MemoryEngine
nodes.summary: Top-level memory engine facade that composes short-term, long-term, working, and episodic memory subsystems plus the summarization pipeline, providing add_message, get_context, and clear_session operations.

nodes.name: safety.py
nodes.summary: Safety engine providing input/output moderation with prompt injection detection, PII detection and redaction, sensitive content filtering, and LLM-based content moderation pipeline.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses regex patterns for PII detection (phone, ID card, bank card, email), configurable SafetyPolicy to toggle individual checks, and optional LLM adapter for deeper injection and moderation analysis.

nodes.name: SafetyAction
nodes.summary: Enum defining safety actions: allow, warn, block.

nodes.name: SafetyPolicy
nodes.summary: Configuration dataclass controlling which safety checks (injection, PII, sensitive, compliance) are enabled and their parameters.

nodes.name: SafetyIssue
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing a single safety issue with type, detail, severity, and recommended action.

nodes.name: SafetyResult
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing the aggregate safety check result with safe flag, issues list, filtered content, action, and reason.

nodes.name: PIIEntity
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing a detected PII entity with its type, value, position range, and masked form.

nodes.name: SafetyEngine
nodes.summary: Core safety engine performing multi-stage input/output moderation: injection detection, PII detection and masking, sensitive word filtering, and optional LLM-based deep moderation.

nodes.name: workflow.py

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README to understand the Agent Engine Platform's purpose and architecture. This is a full-stack AI Agent platform built on FastAPI + Python 3.11 (backend) and Next.js 14 + React 18 (frontend), orchestrated via Docker Compose. It provides end-to-end capabilities: Agent management, multi-model routing, knowledge base engine (RAG), workflow engine (DAG), multi-agent orchestration, tool engine, safety engine, evaluation engine, memory system, MCP service, multi-tenancy, and audit monitoring.

tour.title: Backend Entry Point
tour.description: The backend FastAPI application starts here. main.py configures CORS, registers middleware (request ID, audit, HTTPS redirect), mounts all API v1 routes, sets up error handlers, and manages the startup/shutdown lifecycle including scheduler initialization. This file is the runtime spine of the entire backend -- every request flows through it.
tour.languageLesson: FastAPI uses an event-based lifecycle: the 'startup' event initializes resources (DB pools, scheduler), while 'shutdown' releases them. Middleware registered via @app.middleware executes in LIFO order -- the last registered middleware runs first on the inbound request.

tour.title: Core Infrastructure
tour.description: These four modules form the backbone of every backend operation. database.py provides async SQLAlchemy session management. auth.py handles JWT and API token verification with RBAC. security.py implements password hashing (bcrypt) and symmetric encryption (Fernet) for sensitive data. exceptions.py defines the platform-wide exception hierarchy used across all API and engine layers.
tour.languageLesson: Python's abc.ABC and abstract methods define contracts that all implementations must fulfill. The exception hierarchy here uses inheritance to allow catching broad (AgentEngineError) or specific (RateLimitError) exceptions at different layers -- a pattern called 'exception shielding'.

tour.title: Data Models and API Schemas
tour.description: The platform's data contract lives in these two files. models/base.py defines 46 SQLAlchemy ORM classes covering all domain entities (tenants, users, agents, workflows, conversations, knowledge bases, tools, evaluations). schemas/api.py provides the matching Pydantic request/response models that validate and serialize API payloads. Together they form the bridge between the database and the REST API.
tour.languageLesson: SQLAlchemy async ORM uses DeclarativeBase with Mapped[type] annotations for type-safe column definitions. Pydantic v2 models use model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True) to enable direct ORM-to-schema conversion without manual mapping.

tour.title: REST API Layer
tour.description: All API endpoints are aggregated in the v1 router barrel file and exposed as REST endpoints. Each module handles a domain: agents (CRUD), chat (streaming SSE), knowledge (document upload and processing), workflows (DAG execution), and more. Every endpoint is protected by RBAC permission checks from the auth module in Step 3.

tour.title: Knowledge Engine (RAG)
tour.description: The knowledge engine is the most sophisticated subsystem, implementing a full RAG pipeline. Documents are parsed by format-specific parsers (PDF, Word, Excel, PPT, text), then chunked using recursive, semantic, parent-child, or QA-pair strategies. The RAG pipeline orchestrates embedding, multi-strategy retrieval (vector, graph, hybrid), reranking, and context assembly. Storage spans Milvus (vectors), Neo4j (knowledge graph), and Elasticsearch (full-text search).
tour.languageLesson: RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) combines document retrieval with LLM generation. The dual-level retriever implements LightRAG-style retrieval with four modes: naive (simple vector search), local (entity neighborhood), global (theme-based), and hybrid (combined). This multi-strategy approach significantly improves recall for complex queries.

tour.title: Model Engine and Routing
tour.description: The model engine provides a unified abstraction over multiple LLM providers. base.py defines adapter interfaces (BaseLLMAdapter, BaseEmbeddingAdapter) and configuration dataclasses. router.py implements the routing layer with circuit breaker pattern, weighted and round-robin provider selection, and health-based endpoint filtering for resilient multi-provider failover. cost_tracker.py monitors per-tenant usage costs.
tour.languageLesson: The circuit breaker pattern prevents cascading failures: when an LLM provider fails repeatedly, the breaker 'opens' and routes requests to healthy providers. After a cooldown period, it allows a 'half-open' probe request to test recovery. This is essential for multi-provider resilience.

tour.title: Workflow and Multi-Agent Engines
tour.description: The workflow engine executes DAG-based automation with node types including LLM calls, conditions, parallel branches, loops, HTTP requests, code execution, human approval, and sub-workflows -- complete with retry logic and execution tracing. The multi-agent engine supports two patterns: Crew-based orchestration (sequential, hierarchical, parallel, consensus) and Agent handoff with loop prevention and structured JSON protocol.
tour.languageLesson: DAG (Directed Acyclic Graph) execution requires topological sorting to determine node execution order. Cycles must be detected at validation time, not runtime. The handoff protocol uses max-hops limiting to prevent infinite agent-to-agent delegation loops -- a common failure mode in multi-agent systems.

tour.title: Tool, Safety, Memory, and Eval Engines
tour.description: These four engines complete the platform's AI capabilities. The tool engine provides a singleton registry for builtin and custom tools with OpenAI function-calling schema export and an executor with timeout and permission enforcement. The safety engine performs input/output moderation with prompt injection detection, PII redaction, and content filtering. The memory engine manages short-term, long-term, working, and episodic memory. The eval engine implements Ragas-style metrics for RAG quality assessment.

tour.title: Frontend Architecture
tour.description: The Next.js 14 frontend uses App Router with route groups for auth and platform sections. The platform layout wraps all authenticated pages with a Sidebar, Header, and auth guard. api.ts is the central API client wrapping all backend endpoints. Types are shared via a single definitions file. Zustand stores manage client state for chat and authentication.
tour.languageLesson: Next.js App Router uses route groups like (auth) and (platform) to organize layouts without affecting URL paths. Zustand stores provide lightweight state management with built-in devtools -- much simpler than Redux for medium-complexity apps. The api.ts client centralizes error handling and auth token injection.

tour.title: Frontend Pages and Components
tour.description: The user-facing pages cover all platform features: agent listing, chat interface, knowledge base management, workflow editor, model configuration, and tool testing. The ChatMessage component renders markdown with streaming indicators. The Sidebar provides navigation across all platform sections.

tour.title: Infrastructure and Deployment
tour.description: The platform runs as 9 Docker Compose services: MySQL 8.0, Redis 7, Milvus (vector DB), Neo4j (graph DB), Elasticsearch, the FastAPI backend, Next.js frontend, Nginx reverse proxy, and Celery workers. The backend uses a single-stage Docker build on python:3.11-slim, while the frontend uses a multi-stage build to produce a minimal production image. The .env.example template defines 62 configuration variables across all services.
tour.languageLesson: Multi-stage Docker builds separate compilation from runtime: the builder stage has all dev dependencies (npm packages, build tools), while the final stage only contains compiled artifacts. This can reduce image size by 50-80%. The frontend's Dockerfile uses node:20-alpine for a minimal base, and runs as a non-root user for security.

tour.title: Configuration and MCP Service
tour.description: config.py is the central settings module using pydantic-settings, defining parameters for all external services (MySQL, Redis, Celery, Milvus, Neo4j, Elasticsearch, JWT, CORS). The MCP server exposes the platform's capabilities as tools via JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdio -- enabling external AI agents to create agents, search knowledge, run workflows, and send messages programmatically.
tour.languageLesson: MCP (Model Context Protocol) is an emerging standard for AI tool integration. The server exposes tools as JSON-RPC methods over stdio transport, making the platform's capabilities accessible to any MCP-compatible AI agent. pydantic-settings automatically loads values from environment variables, .env files, and validates types -- eliminating manual config parsing.
